title: ceratobium antennatum
intro: Ceratobium antennatum, also known as the Giant Stinging Tree, is a species of plant that is native to Queensland in Australia and is characterized by its large, stinging leaves. The plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, which can be used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Ceratobium antennatum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared for herbal use in various forms, including a tincture or a salve, which can be applied topically to alleviate symptoms and promote healing.
